findings.summary: Animated MOV and static fallback PNG are NOT in bundle.resources — the next CI build will ship without them and EVERY installed free user silently falls through to the legacy static wordmark.
findings.evidence: tauri.conf.json bundle.resources lists liquid-clips-wordmark.png but not assets/watermark/made-with-liquid-clips.mov or assets/watermark/made-with-liquid-clips-static.png. Verified: `ls /Applications/Liquid Clips.app/Contents/Resources/_up_/python-sidecar/assets/watermark/` → no such directory in the current install. After a CI rebuild, Path(__file__).resolve().parent / 'assets' / 'watermark' / 'made-with-liquid-clips.mov' resolves under Contents/Resources/_up_/python-sidecar/assets/watermark/ which never gets populated.
findings.reproduce: grep -n 'watermark\|made-with' desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json   # zero hits in bundle.resources
findings.suggested_fix: Add to bundle.resources: '../python-sidecar/assets/watermark/made-with-liquid-clips.mov' and '../python-sidecar/assets/watermark/made-with-liquid-clips-static.png'. Verify in CI by listing Contents/Resources/_up_/python-sidecar/assets/watermark/ after build. Without this fix the entire animated-overlay commit is dead code on shipped builds — only Daniel's dev tree will ever see it.

findings.summary: clip['start'] / clip['end'] can be None (desktop emits null for unset). float(None) raises TypeError BEFORE the dispatcher is called, crashing the entire stage_reframe loop — not just the watermark.
findings.evidence: Line 1326-1328: `_clip_seconds = max(0.1, float(clip.get('end', 0)) - float(clip.get('start', 0)))`. If clip.get('start') returns Python None (which it will if the key exists with a null value — `.get('start', 0)` only substitutes when the key is ABSENT, not when it's present-and-None), float(None) raises TypeError. The try/except at line 1623 only wraps the animated-filter construction inside `_watermark_filter`, NOT this enclosing arithmetic which runs first.
findings.suggested_fix: Coerce with explicit None-default: `float(clip.get('start') or 0)` and same for end. Or wrap the duration derivation in its own try/except that falls through to static. The downstream pattern at line 2207 has the same latent bug — fix both for consistency (pattern-grep follow-up).

findings.summary: Empty/corrupt MOV passes Path.exists() but ffmpeg errors mid-encode — try/except only wraps filter-string CONSTRUCTION, not the eventual ffmpeg invocation, so the export crashes with a generic ffmpeg error instead of falling back to static.
findings.evidence: Line 1612: `if clip_seconds >= 2.5 and animated_path.exists():` then line 1620: `try: return _made_with_animated_watermark_filter(...)`. The animated function returns a string — it cannot fail on a corrupt MOV. The actual file-read happens later inside the ffmpeg subprocess (when the -vf chain runs the `movie=` filter), which is OUTSIDE this try/except. A 0-byte or partial MOV → Path.exists()=True → filter string returned → ffmpeg subprocess fails → entire export fails. The fallback path (_liquid_lift_watermark_filter) is never reached.
findings.reproduce: truncate -s 0 desktop/python-sidecar/assets/watermark/made-with-liquid-clips.mov; export a 5s clip → ffmpeg error, no static fallback fires.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) sanity-check the MOV before returning the filter (size > 1KB AND ffprobe stream count >= 1, cached so it doesn't run per-clip), or (b) document this gap in WATERMARK_ACCEPTANCE.md fallback section and accept it (the cosmetic alternative is the export fails — also a P0 UX outcome). The acceptance doc fallback test (`mv .mov{,.disabled}`) catches the missing case but not the corrupt case.

findings.summary: When out_w or out_h come back 0/None (cap_size probe failure), wm_w clamps to 280 — wider than the output frame — and ffmpeg's overlay either clips silently or errors. No early-return guard.
findings.evidence: Line 1664-1666: `wm_w = max(280, int(out_w * 0.32))`. int(0 * 0.32) = 0, max(280, 0) = 280. If out_w==0, overlay is 280px wide on a 0px frame → ffmpeg `overlay=W-w-x:H-h-y` with negative coords. int(None * 0.32) → TypeError, but caught by the try/except wrapper (good). int(0 * 0.32) goes through silently — bad.
findings.reproduce: Direct call: `_made_with_animated_watermark_filter(0, 0, 5.0, Path('/tmp/x.mov'))` returns a filter string with `overlay=W-w-36:H-h-72` and `scale=280:-2`. Pipe into ffmpeg on a 0-frame source → likely error or zero-pixel paint.
findings.suggested_fix: Add early bail in _watermark_filter: `if not out_w or not out_h or out_w < 100 or out_h < 100: return _liquid_lift_watermark_filter(out_w, out_h)`. (Legacy static path has the same vulnerability but is pre-existing; flag for the static path's audit too.)

findings.summary: movie= filter path is NOT shell-quoted or backslash-escaped. Works in my smoke test for spaces, but a colon in the path (rare but possible — e.g. Time Machine snapshot paths, user-renamed Applications) WILL break parsing because ffmpeg uses `:` as the option separator inside movie=.
findings.evidence: Line 1672-1675 produces `movie={overlay_path}:loop=0,...`. Smoke test with `/tmp/Liquid Clips Test/wm.mov` succeeded (8 fuchsia pixels in br region of probe frame), but a path like `/Volumes/Backup:foo/.../wm.mov` would be parsed as `movie=/Volumes/Backup` with option `foo/.../wm.mov` and option `loop=0`. The same risk applies to `\`, `'`, `[`, `]` in paths.
findings.suggested_fix: ffmpeg `movie=` supports `\:` escaping for literal colons. Either escape with `str(overlay_path).replace(':', '\\:').replace("'", "\\'")` or wrap the option in `\'...\'` quotes per ffmpeg filter syntax. Acceptable to defer if the asset path is repo-controlled — but a TimeMachine restore or unusual mount could move it. Document the assumption.

findings.summary: Component is exported but has zero call-sites in the codebase. Either the in-app surfaces (Splash / About / end-card) listed in the file header aren't wired yet, or the component is dead-code-shipped.
findings.evidence: grep -rn 'MadeWithLiquidClips' desktop/src/ → only the component definition. No imports. Acceptance check #4 ('React preview ↔ exported MP4 presence match') REQUIRES this component be mounted somewhere observable, otherwise the visual agreement test has nothing to test.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) wire it into Splash / About / end-card now so acceptance #4 has a surface to verify, or (b) drop this commit's component until those surfaces are scoped. Shipping unused exported React in src/ adds bundle bytes without payoff.

findings.summary: Just-upgraded user keeps watermark for up to 10 minutes due to the _WATERMARK_TIER_CACHE TTL — this IS a P0 UX risk for the conversion flow, not a footnote. A user paying £/$ and seeing the same watermark on their next export will rage-message support.
findings.evidence: stages.py:1511 `_WATERMARK_CACHE_TTL_S = 600`. Acceptance #3 documents this with 'wait 10 minutes OR restart' as the workaround. There is no cache-invalidation hook from the upgrade success path back into the sidecar. The acceptance doc treats this as expected behavior.
findings.reproduce: Sign in as free → export 5s clip → see watermark → complete upgrade via /upgrade → export same clip within 10 min → watermark STILL present.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sidecar RPC `clear_watermark_cache` or simply have the desktop call _should_watermark cache eviction after a successful upgrade webhook. Or: shorten TTL to 60s for free-tier (paid is cached forever already since features.watermark=False). The conversion-event is rare; cache miss cost is one extra /sync per export which is bounded.

findings.summary: Comment says 'Filter-string construction shouldn't fail at runtime' — when the assumption is THAT confident, the try/except adds noise rather than safety. Either remove the try/except (and accept a single failure path) or document a real failure mode it actually catches.
findings.evidence: Lines 1620-1632. The function body only does Path arithmetic and f-string composition, neither of which throw under normal Python operation. The comment admits this. The except clause uses `Exception as exc` which (correctly) excludes KeyboardInterrupt — flagged below as good. But the safety net catches almost nothing real.
findings.suggested_fix: Either drop the try/except (Karpathy: simpler wins), or expand the wrapped scope to ALSO include the Path.exists() probe + a 1KB+ size check so the wrapper genuinely catches the disk-failure mode P1-003 calls out.

findings.summary: ariaLabel default is 'Made with Liquid Clips'; if a consumer passes ariaLabel='' (per the JSDoc invitation 'on decorative-only mounts'), the resulting `alt=''` plus `role='img'` is screen-reader noise — role=img on an empty-alt image is an unannounced presentational element, but role=img demands a label. Conflict.
findings.evidence: Lines 41 + 50. ariaLabel='' → . Per ARIA: role='img' requires aria-label OR alt. alt='' satisfies it nominally but axe-core warns. The JSDoc tells consumers to do this on decorative mounts.
findings.suggested_fix: When ariaLabel === '', drop role='img' and rely on alt=''. Or document that decorative mounts should set aria-hidden='true' instead.

findings.summary: 8.6MB MOV shipped in every install for a 480×120 12s loop is high — render_watermark_overlay.py exists and bundled ffmpeg is present, so first-launch render is a viable alternative. Defer until DMG ship-cost is a real complaint.
findings.evidence: du -sh: 8.6M for the watermark dir. render_watermark_overlay.py builds the MOV in ~3s on a 2020 MBP per its docstring. ffmpeg is bundled at python-sidecar/bin/. Alternative: ship the 78KB bug PNG only and lazy-render the MOV on first export.
findings.suggested_fix: Defer — 8.6MB on top of ~155MB ffmpeg+whisper baseline is 5% increase, not a blocker. Note for v0.7.6x if DMG size becomes a complaint. Don't change in this commit.

findings.summary: `except Exception as exc` — correctly excludes BaseException (SIGINT/SystemExit propagate). The user's audit asked to verify this; it's right. Flagging as confirmed-good, not a finding.
findings.evidence: Line 1623: `except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001`. Exception is the right base — KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit derive from BaseException, not Exception, so Ctrl-C still aborts the export. Matches user's intent.

findings.summary: SVG uses only canonical fuchsia #FF1A8C, ink #F4F1EA, and black/white rgba derivatives — no drift. Brand kit gate IG-012 passes.
findings.evidence: grep -oE '#[0-9A-Fa-f]{3,8}|rgba?\([^)]*\)' → only #F4F1EA, #FF1A8C, rgba(0,0,0,0.5), rgba(244,241,234,0.7), rgba(255,26,140,0.55|0.95). All derive from the three canonical tokens.

findings.summary: No IRON GATE sentinels exist near stages.py:1316 or in desktop/src/components/brand/. No locked contracts touched.
findings.evidence: grep -n 'IRON GATE' desktop/python-sidecar/stages.py → 0 hits. grep -rn 'IRON GATE' desktop/src/components/brand/ → 0 hits. IG-012 (brand kit) is contract-only and is satisfied per P2-012.
findings.suggested_fix: No action — no sentinels at risk.

smoke_tests_run.name: ffmpeg-bundled-prores-decode
smoke_tests_run.output: desktop/python-sidecar/bin/ffmpeg 8.1.1-tessus (x86_64) supports prores decode + encode (prores_ks). MOV decode path works.

smoke_tests_run.name: path-with-spaces-overlay
smoke_tests_run.output: ffmpeg movie=/tmp/Liquid Clips Test/wm.mov SUCCEEDED — output frame contains 9 fuchsia pixels in bottom-right region. Unescaped spaces are tolerated by the bundled ffmpeg's movie= parser.

smoke_tests_run.name: bundle-resources-check
smoke_tests_run.output: tauri.conf.json bundle.resources missing the new MOV + static PNG. /Applications/Liquid Clips.app/Contents/Resources/_up_/python-sidecar/assets/watermark/ does NOT exist on the currently-installed app. Next CI build will not ship the animated overlay → silent fallback to static for EVERY shipped user.

smoke_tests_run.name: brand-kit-drift
smoke_tests_run.output: SVG uses only #FF1A8C, #F4F1EA, and rgba derivatives — IG-012 satisfied.

smoke_tests_run.name: react-component-call-sites
smoke_tests_run.output: MadeWithLiquidClips has zero importers in desktop/src/. Component is defined but never mounted.
